definition: Heart condition that develops after birth (infection, inflammation, etc.) May involve sudden onset or traumatic medical events
Minimally invasive procedure using tubes to diagnose or repair heart defects Still stressful and can involve uncertainty about outcomes
Cardiac-specific intensive care unit Repeated admissions common; families often experience chronic stress
Heart condition present at birth affecting structure or function Lifelong condition; often involves repeated medical experiences and chronic uncertainty
Routine tracking of a child’s weight to monitor growth and fluid status Small changes can feel high-stakes; can create stress and fear around feeding and health stability

Medical treatment that removes waste and extra fluid when kidneys are not functioning properly Often used in critical illness; can signal high medical complexity and prolonged hospitalization, contributing to caregiver stress and trauma
Ultrasound used to view heart structure and blood flow Requires stillness; can be distressing for young children
Life support machine that takes over heart and lung function Highly traumatic visual and emotional experience for families
Test measuring heart’s electrical activity Often repeated; can create anxiety due to medical setting
Feeding tube placed directly into the stomach Can impact feeding, development, and caregiver stress
Replacement of a failing heart with a donor heart Can involve complex emotions including fear, guilt, and identity shifts
Underdevelopment of the left side of the heart One of the most severe forms; requires multiple surgeries and long-term care
Device that detects and corrects dangerous heart rhythms Shocks can be physically and emotionally distressing
inserted through the nose for feeding Can be uncomfortable and distressing; may create sensory aversions
Intensive care unit for newborns Early separation and medical trauma can impact attachment
requiring the chest to be opened and use of a heart-lung machine Highly traumatic experience for both child and caregivers; often repeated
Device that regulates heart rhythm Child may be aware of device; can impact body awareness and identity
Intensive care unit for children High-stress environment with frequent alarms and interventions
A device that measures oxygen levels in the blood using a sensor (usually on finger or toe) Families may monitor frequently; can increase anxiety and hypervigilance around numbers
Complex condition involving four heart defects affecting oxygen levels Often involves early surgery and visible symptoms (e.g., blue skin), which can impact identity
Mechanical pump that supports heart function Often used in severe cases; may indicate high medical complexity
Hole between lower heart chambers May involve early monitoring or surgery; family may have experienced prolonged stress
